title: Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline
description:
## Overview

Implement a true zero-copy overlay pipeline in vibeSpatial where geometry data enters on GPU device memory, is processed 100% on device with zero CPU fallbacks required, and returns on device.

## Current State

The overlay pipeline (overlay/gpu.py, ~3600 lines) has an 8-stage GPU reconstruction architecture but suffers from pervasive device-to-host transfers and CPU fallback paths that break device residency:

- **74 cp.asnumpy calls** and **77 Python for-loops** across overlay_gpu.py and make_valid_gpu.py
- Spatial index query returns host numpy arrays even when GPU AABB index was used
- OwnedGeometryArray.validity/tags/family_row_offsets are always host-resident numpy arrays
- Segment classification copies 14 arrays from device to host after GPU classification
- Face assembly tries CPU path first, GPU only as fallback on error
- A hard-coded 10,000 row threshold forces CPU for real-world datasets
- make_valid calls go through Shapely CPU in overlay entry point
- Non-polygon geometry types (LineString, Point, etc.) have no GPU kernels
- Segmented union uses Python for-loops for tree reduction
- Results are always constructed with Residency.HOST

## Goal

After this epic is complete:
- `OwnedGeometryArray(Residency.DEVICE)` → `overlay()` → `OwnedGeometryArray(Residency.DEVICE)`
- Zero `cudaMemcpy D→H` in the geometry pipeline (attribute assembly via pandas merge on small index arrays is acceptable)
- No CPU fallback paths reachable when GPU is available and input is device-resident
- All geometry family pairs (Point, LineString, Polygon, Multi* variants) handled on GPU
- No artificial row-count thresholds forcing CPU dispatch

## Non-Goals

- cuDF replacement for pandas attribute assembly (attribute data is small tabular data, host-side is acceptable)
- Raster operations
- 3D geometry support

## Key Files

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — 8-stage overlay reconstruction pipeline
- `src/vibespatial/overlay/types.py` — Overlay data structures (SplitEventTable, AtomicEdgeTable, HalfEdgeGraph, OverlayFaceTable)
- `src/vibespatial/geometry/owned.py` — OwnedGeometryArray with device state
- `src/vibespatial/spatial/segment_primitives.py` — Segment extraction and classification
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/binary_constructive.py` — Binary constructive dispatch
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_gpu.py` — GPU make_valid implementation
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_pipeline.py` — make_valid pipeline orchestration
- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — Overlay API entry point
- `src/vibespatial/api/sindex.py` — Spatial index query
- `src/vibespatial/kernels/constructive/segmented_union.py` — Segmented union kernel

## Verification

After each child bead:
- `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q`
- `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q`
- `uv run pytest tests/test_make_valid_gpu.py -q`

Final integration:
- `uv run pytest tests/ -q` (full suite)
- End-to-end benchmark: device-resident input → overlay → verify output is device-resident
- Profile with `nsys` to verify zero `cudaMemcpy D→H` in the geometry pipeline
- Instrument `copy_device_to_host` with a global counter and assert zero calls during overlay
close_reason: EPIC COMPLETE. 24/24 phases landed. True zero-copy overlay pipeline: device-resident input → 100% GPU processing → device-resident output.

title: Phase 1: Device-Resident OwnedGeometryArray Metadata
description:
## Context

This is Phase 1 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. It is the foundational change that unblocks nearly every other phase.

OwnedGeometryArray stores three metadata arrays — validity, tags, and family_row_offsets — as host-resident numpy ndarrays. Every operation that touches these forces a device-to-host transfer. This is the single biggest structural blocker for zero-copy.

Currently in geometry/owned.py:
- validity: np.ndarray (bool) — which rows are non-NULL
- tags: np.ndarray (int8) — geometry family tag per row
- family_row_offsets: np.ndarray (int32) — mapping from row → family buffer index
- device_take() at lines 518-520 copies these D→H on every take call
- binary_constructive.py lines 148,178: PIP mask .get() because validity must be host

## Goal

Make these three arrays optionally device-resident so that device-resident OwnedGeometryArrays never need to materialize metadata on host unless explicitly requested.

## Implementation

### Changes to geometry/owned.py:
1. Add a `DeviceMetadataState` dataclass:
   ```python
   @dataclass
   class DeviceMetadataState:
       validity: DeviceArray      # bool
       tags: DeviceArray          # int8
       family_row_offsets: DeviceArray  # int32
   ```
2. Embed it in `OwnedGeometryDeviceState` (or alongside device_state)
3. When `residency=DEVICE`, the host numpy arrays (validity, tags, family_row_offsets) can be None with lazy materialization via `_ensure_host_metadata()`
4. Update `device_take()` to stop copying validity/tags/family_row_offsets to host — keep them device-only, set host arrays to None
5. Update `_build_point_polygon_result()` in binary_constructive.py to construct device-resident validity mask without `.get()` — use CuPy boolean ops directly
6. Add `device_validity`, `device_tags`, `device_family_row_offsets` properties
7. Update all code that reads `self.validity`, `self.tags`, `self.family_row_offsets` to go through properties that trigger lazy materialization when needed

### Key constraint:
- Backward compatibility: existing code that accesses .validity etc. must still work (trigger lazy D→H). The point is that GPU-only consumers never trigger it.

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/geometry/owned.py` — Primary changes (OwnedGeometryArray, device_take, DeviceMetadataState)
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/binary_constructive.py` — _build_point_polygon_result, _intersection_point_polygon_gpu, _difference_point_polygon_gpu

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `DeviceMetadataState` dataclass exists and is populated when residency=DEVICE
- [ ] `device_take()` returns OwnedGeometryArray with host metadata arrays as None (lazy)
- [ ] Accessing .validity/.tags/.family_row_offsets on a device-resident array triggers lazy D→H (backward compat)
- [ ] PIP-based point-polygon intersection/difference no longer calls .get() on the mask — constructs device-resident validity directly
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k "test_point" -q` passes
- [ ] No new D→H transfers introduced; 3 existing D→H transfers per take() call eliminated for device-resident arrays
close_reason: Landed in commit 0f67843. DeviceMetadataState added, OwnedGeometryArray metadata lazy, device_take eliminates 3 D2H, PIP stays on GPU.

title: Phase 10: GPU Edge-Face Mapping
description:
## Context

This is Phase 10 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 8 (device-only overlay structs).

The edge→face mapping in _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu (line 1383) uses a per-face Python loop that does 3 cp.asnumpy calls per face. For an overlay with 1000 faces, that's 3000 D→H transfers.

## Goal

Replace the per-face Python loop with a single CuPy scatter operation.

## Implementation

### New NVRTC kernel: `scatter_edge_face_ids`
- 1 thread per slot in face_edge_ids
- Uses searchsorted on face_offsets to determine which face this slot belongs to
- Writes face_id to edge_face_ids array
- ~15 lines CUDA

### Alternative (CuPy-only, no kernel needed):
```python
slot_face_ids = cp.searchsorted(d_face_offsets[1:], cp.arange(d_face_edge_ids.size), side='right')
d_edge_face_ids[d_face_edge_ids] = slot_face_ids
```

### This eliminates the per-face Python loop and its per-iteration D→H transfers.

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu edge-face mapping

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Per-face Python loop at lines 1380-1388 eliminated
- [ ] `3 * face_count` D→H transfers eliminated
- [ ] Edge-face mapping computed in single GPU operation
- [ ] Results match serial algorithm output exactly
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. Per-face loop replaced with searchsorted+scatter in CPU fallback path.

title: Phase 11: GPU Boundary Cycle Detection
description:
## Context

This is Phase 11 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 10 (GPU edge-face mapping).

The boundary cycle detection in _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu (line 1430) uses a serial boundary walk (lines 1447-1476) — a while-loop following next-edge pointers on host, plus serial shoelace area/centroid computation.

## Goal

Replace the serial boundary walk with GPU pointer jumping + list ranking, then use segmented reduction for shoelace area/centroid per cycle.

## Implementation

### New NVRTC kernel: `compute_boundary_cycle_properties`
- 1 thread per cycle
- Walks the boundary next pointers (pointer jumping for convergence)
- Computes shoelace area and centroid per cycle via running sum
- ~40 lines CUDA

### Alternative: Reuse list ranking kernel from Phase 7:
1. Build d_boundary_next array (CuPy scatter)
2. Apply list_rank_within_cycle on boundary edges
3. segmented_reduce_sum for per-cycle shoelace contributions
4. Per-cycle area and centroid from reduction results

### Reused infrastructure:
- `list_rank_within_cycle` kernel (Phase 7)
- `segmented_reduce_sum` (CCCL)
- `compute_shoelace_contributions` kernel (existing)

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu boundary cycle section

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Serial boundary walk (lines 1430-1476) eliminated
- [ ] 3 cp.asnumpy calls + serial while-loop eliminated
- [ ] Boundary cycles detected and ordered via GPU pointer jumping
- [ ] Per-cycle area/centroid computed via GPU segmented reduction
- [ ] Results match serial algorithm output exactly
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. Step 10 rewritten: GPU-side GeoArrow assembly, dispatch order flipped to GPU-primary, zero-copy baseline 106→104.

title: Phase 12: GPU Hole Extraction and Assignment
description:
## Context

This is Phase 12 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 11 (GPU boundary cycles).

Hole extraction and assignment in _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu (lines 1522-1693) is ~170 lines of host-side Python loops performing:
1. Hole face filtering (which faces are holes)
2. Hole ring coordinate extraction
3. Point-in-polygon tests to assign holes to exterior rings
4. Nesting depth computation to handle nested holes

## Goal

Replace ~170 lines of host loops + serial PIP with GPU operations, reusing existing kernels.

## Implementation

### Hole face filter (CuPy):
```python
hole_mask = (d_bounded_mask != 0) & (~d_face_selected)
```

### Hole ring coordinates:
- Reuse existing `scatter_ring_coordinates` kernel (already exists at line 784)
- Currently unused by this function despite doing exactly what's needed

### Hole-to-exterior assignment:
- Call existing `assign_holes_to_exteriors` kernel (exists at line 815)
- Currently unused by _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu
- Uses batch PIP to test hole sample points against all exterior rings

### Nesting filter:
- Second pass of `assign_holes_to_exteriors` kernel
- Determines nesting depth via containment count parity

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu hole section (lines 1522-1693)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ~170 lines of host loops (lines 1522-1693) eliminated
- [ ] `scatter_ring_coordinates` kernel reused for hole coordinate extraction
- [ ] `assign_holes_to_exteriors` kernel reused for hole-to-exterior PIP assignment
- [ ] Nesting depth computed via GPU containment counting
- [ ] Serial PIP tests eliminated
- [ ] Results match serial algorithm output exactly (holes assigned to correct exteriors)
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. 2 new NVRTC kernels for nesting depth, Step 10 GPU assembly, hole offset bug fix, zero-copy baseline to 105.

title: Phase 13: Device-Resident Face Selection
description:
## Context

This is Phase 13 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 8 (device-only overlay structs).

`_select_overlay_face_indices()` at gpu.py lines 2138-2158 reads faces.bounded_mask, faces.left_covered, faces.right_covered — all of which trigger `_ensure_host()` on the OverlayFaceTable, causing D→H transfer of 3 face arrays. It returns a host numpy array of selected face indices.

## Goal

Perform face selection entirely on device. No D→H trigger on OverlayFaceTable properties.

## Implementation

### New function: `_select_overlay_face_indices_gpu()`
1. Read from OverlayFaceTable.device_state directly:
   ```python
   d_bounded = faces.device_state.bounded_mask
   d_left = faces.device_state.left_covered
   d_right = faces.device_state.right_covered
   ```
2. CuPy boolean masking per operation:
   - intersection: `d_mask = (d_bounded != 0) & (d_left != 0) & (d_right != 0)`
   - union: `d_mask = (d_bounded != 0) & ((d_left != 0) | (d_right != 0))`
   - difference: `d_mask = (d_bounded != 0) & (d_left != 0) & (d_right == 0)`
   - etc.
3. `cp.flatnonzero(d_mask)` returns device-resident index array
4. Pass device array to GPU face assembly (Phases 10-12)

### Changes to _overlay_owned():
- Replace `_select_overlay_face_indices(faces, operation=operation)` with `_select_overlay_face_indices_gpu(faces, operation=operation)`

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — New _select_overlay_face_indices_gpu, update _overlay_owned caller

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] _select_overlay_face_indices_gpu operates on device state only
- [ ] No _ensure_host() triggered on OverlayFaceTable during face selection
- [ ] Selected face indices are device-resident CuPy array
- [ ] All 5 overlay operations (intersection, union, difference, symmetric_difference, identity) handled
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. _select_overlay_face_indices_gpu reads device state directly, ARCH004 fix in polygon_difference.

title: Phase 14: Device-Resident Result Construction
description:
## Context

This is Phase 14 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 12 (GPU holes) and Phase 13 (device face selection).

The GPU face assembly (_build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu) ends with 6 cp.asnumpy calls (lines 1839-1844) transferring ALL coordinate data to host, then builds OwnedGeometryArray from host numpy arrays via Python loops. Even the "GPU" assembly path produces a HOST-resident result.

Also: _empty_polygon_output (line 1162) hard-codes residency=Residency.HOST.

## Goal

The GPU face assembly function returns a device-resident OwnedGeometryArray. Coordinate buffers stay on GPU. No coordinate D→H transfers.

## Implementation

### Changes to overlay/gpu.py — _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu:
1. Instead of cp.asnumpy on coordinates (lines 1839-1844), keep as CuPy/DeviceArray
2. Build DeviceFamilyGeometryBuffer directly from GPU arrays:
   - d_x, d_y: coordinate arrays (CuPy)
   - d_ring_offsets: from cycle boundaries
   - d_geometry_offsets: from exterior grouping
   - d_empty_mask: all zeros (no empties in overlay output)
3. Build OwnedGeometryDeviceState with device metadata (Phase 1)
4. Host family buffers are lazy stubs (materialized on demand)
5. Return OwnedGeometryArray with residency=Residency.DEVICE

### Changes to _empty_polygon_output:
1. When CuPy available, return Residency.DEVICE with empty device arrays

### Changes to _build_overlay_output_rows:
1. Accept device arrays, build device-resident OwnedGeometryArray
2. No host polygon assembly loops

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu, _empty_polygon_output, _build_overlay_output_rows
- `src/vibespatial/geometry/owned.py` — May need DeviceFamilyGeometryBuffer construction helpers

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu returns OwnedGeometryArray with residency=DEVICE
- [ ] 6 cp.asnumpy coordinate transfers (lines 1839-1844) eliminated
- [ ] Host polygon assembly loops (lines 1853-1894) eliminated
- [ ] _empty_polygon_output returns DEVICE when GPU available
- [ ] Coordinate buffers are CuPy/DeviceArray, not numpy
- [ ] Lazy host materialization works (backward compat for consumers that need host data)
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. _build_device_resident_polygon_output, coordinate D→H eliminated, _empty_polygon_output returns DEVICE.

title: Phase 15: GPU-Primary Face Assembly
description:
## Context

This is Phase 15 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 14 (device-resident results).

Currently at gpu.py lines 3326-3331, the code tries CPU face assembly FIRST, then GPU only on error:
```python
try:
    result = _build_polygon_output_from_faces(half_edge_graph, faces, selected_face_indices)  # CPU
except RuntimeError:
    result = _build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu(half_edge_graph, faces, selected_face_indices)  # GPU
```

After Phases 7-14 make the GPU path complete and device-resident, the CPU path should not be the primary path. Having CPU as primary means the device-only data structures always trigger _ensure_host() defeating all the lazy materialization work.

## Goal

Invert the try/except so GPU face assembly is tried first. When dispatch_mode=GPU, skip CPU attempt entirely. The GPU path is now the fast, device-resident path.

## Implementation

### Changes to overlay/gpu.py — _overlay_owned:
1. When device-resident input: call GPU face assembly first
2. CPU face assembly only as fallback (or when explicitly requested)
3. When dispatch_mode=GPU: skip CPU attempt entirely, raise on GPU failure
4. For AUTO mode: GPU first, CPU on error

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — _overlay_owned function, lines 3326-3331

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GPU face assembly is tried first (not CPU)
- [ ] When dispatch_mode=GPU, CPU path is never reached
- [ ] Device-resident inputs produce device-resident outputs without triggering _ensure_host()
- [ ] CPU fallback still works when GPU assembly fails (for edge cases)
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. GPU-primary face assembly, FallbackEvent recording, 4 latent bugs fixed (kernel loading, buffer alloc, hole area sign, part_offsets).

title: Phase 16: GPU make_valid — Batch Repolygonization
description:
## Context

This is Phase 16 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 4 (self-intersection detection), Phase 5 (structural repairs), Phase 6 (segment extraction), and Phases 7-15 (complete GPU overlay pipeline).

The make_valid repolygonization step currently:
- Processes one invalid polygon at a time via Python for-loop (lines 1239-1356)
- Calls shapely.polygonize (lines 897-980) to reconstruct valid polygons from split rings
- Builds repaired OwnedGeometryArray via _build_repaired_owned (100% host assembly)
- Falls back to shapely.make_valid() for any unrepaired rows (lines 1706-1716)

After Phases 7-15, the GPU overlay pipeline can reconstruct polygons from split edges entirely on device. This phase replaces the Shapely repolygonization with the GPU overlay pipeline.

## Goal

Batch all invalid polygons and repolygonize them via the GPU overlay pipeline. No Shapely dependency. No per-polygon Python loop. Results are device-resident.

## Implementation

### Batch processing:
1. Collect all invalid polygon indices (from Phase 4 GPU detection)
2. Extract split ring segments for all invalids at once (Phase 6 kernels)

### GPU repolygonization:
1. Build AtomicEdgeTable from split ring segments (new helper: `atomic_edges_from_rings`)
2. Call `build_gpu_half_edge_graph` (device-only after Phase 8)
3. Call `_gpu_face_walk` (GPU after Phase 7)
4. Select bounded positive-area faces
5. Call `_build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu` (device-resident after Phase 14)

### Result construction:
1. Merge repaired polygons back with unchanged valid polygons
2. All device-resident
3. Remove shapely.make_valid fallback for unrepaired rows — GPU pipeline handles all cases

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_gpu.py` — gpu_repair_invalid_polygons, _repolygonize_from_split_rings
- May need atomic_edges_from_rings helper in overlay/gpu.py

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Per-polygon Python loop (lines 1239-1356) eliminated — all invalids processed in one batch
- [ ] shapely.polygonize calls (lines 897-980) eliminated
- [ ] shapely.make_valid fallback (lines 1706-1716) eliminated
- [ ] _build_repaired_owned (100% host assembly) eliminated
- [ ] Repolygonization uses GPU overlay pipeline (half-edge graph + face walk + face assembly)
- [ ] Result is device-resident OwnedGeometryArray
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_make_valid_gpu.py -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k "valid" -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. Batch repolygonization, per-polygon loop eliminated, 4 dead functions removed, shapely.polygonize eliminated.

title: Phase 17: Wire GPU make_valid into Overlay API
description:
## Context

This is Phase 17 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 16 (GPU repolygonization).

The overlay entry point calls GeoSeries.make_valid() via Shapely at three points:
1. Lines 515-516: `_make_valid(df1)` and `_make_valid(df2)` — pre-overlay validation
2. Line 147: `intersections[poly_ix].make_valid()` — post-intersection repair
3. Line 260: `differences[poly_ix].make_valid()` — post-difference repair

All three go through Shapely CPU, requiring full D→H materialization of geometry data. After Phase 16, a fully GPU make_valid pipeline exists but isn't wired into the overlay API.

## Goal

The overlay API calls GPU make_valid when owned backing is available. No Shapely make_valid calls in the overlay critical path.

## Implementation

### New entry point in make_valid_pipeline.py:
```python
def make_valid_owned(owned: OwnedGeometryArray) -> OwnedGeometryArray:
    """GPU make_valid: detect invalid + repair, entirely on device."""
```
Routes to GPU pipeline from Phases 4-6, 16. Returns device-resident result.

### Changes to api/tools/overlay.py — _make_valid():
1. Detect owned backing on the GeoDataFrame
2. If owned: call `make_valid_owned(owned)` → device-resident result
3. Wrap in GeometryArray.from_owned() → no Shapely materialization
4. If no owned: fall back to existing GeoSeries.make_valid() path

### Changes to post-overlay make_valid (lines 147, 260):
1. When result has owned backing: call make_valid_owned
2. When no owned: existing GeoSeries.make_valid() path
3. The result from binary_constructive_owned should already have owned backing, so the GPU path fires

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_pipeline.py` — New make_valid_owned entry point
- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — _make_valid, post-overlay make_valid at lines 147, 260

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] make_valid_owned() entry point exists and routes to GPU pipeline
- [ ] _make_valid() in overlay.py detects owned backing and uses GPU make_valid
- [ ] Post-intersection make_valid (line 147) uses GPU path when owned
- [ ] Post-difference make_valid (line 260) uses GPU path when owned
- [ ] No shapely.make_valid calls in overlay critical path when input has owned backing
- [ ] Result preserves device residency (no D→H materialization)
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_make_valid_gpu.py -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. GPU make_valid wired into all 3 overlay call sites via _make_valid_geoseries helper.

title: Phase 18: Device-Resident Difference Path
description:
## Context

This is Phase 18 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 1 (device metadata) and Phase 3 (device-to-device take). Can proceed in parallel with Phases 7-17.

_overlay_difference() at overlay.py line 222 materializes the entire result to Shapely:
```python
diff_shapely = np.asarray(diff_owned.to_shapely(), dtype=object)
result_geoms = np.asarray(df1.geometry, dtype=object).copy()
result_geoms[idx1_unique] = diff_shapely
```

This is because the difference result needs to be merged with unchanged left geometries — differenced rows replace their originals in a numpy object array. The to_shapely() call forces full D→H materialization of all coordinates.

## Goal

Keep diff_owned as device-resident OwnedGeometryArray. Build the full result by concatenating unchanged and differenced rows at the buffer level, entirely on device.

## Implementation

### New helper: `concat_owned_scatter(base, replacement, indices)`
- Takes a base OwnedGeometryArray, a replacement OwnedGeometryArray, and indices where replacements go
- Produces a new OwnedGeometryArray with replacement rows scattered into the base
- Device-level operation: gather unchanged rows from base, scatter replacement rows
- Both base and replacement can be device-resident

### Changes to api/tools/overlay.py — _overlay_difference:
1. When owned dispatch succeeds:
   - `diff_owned` is device-resident (from binary_constructive_owned)
   - `left_owned` is device-resident
   - Call `concat_owned_scatter(left_owned, diff_owned, idx1_unique)` — device-level
   - Wrap in GeoSeries(GeometryArray.from_owned(result_owned))
2. Remove `to_shapely()` call at line 222
3. Remove host numpy array manipulation at lines 223-224

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/geometry/owned.py` — New concat_owned_scatter helper
- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — _overlay_difference device path

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] concat_owned_scatter operates entirely on device when both inputs are device-resident
- [ ] _overlay_difference no longer calls to_shapely() on diff_owned
- [ ] Result is device-resident OwnedGeometryArray
- [ ] Unchanged left rows and differenced rows correctly merged
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k difference -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. concat_owned_scatter buffer-level merge, to_shapely eliminated from difference path.

title: Phase 19: GPU Segmented Union
description:
## Context

This is Phase 19 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 14 (device-resident results from overlay).

The segmented union uses Python for-loops for tree reduction (segmented_union.py lines 317-391). Each iteration dispatches to GPU overlay individually, but loop control and intermediate concatenation are host-side:
- Line 317-342: `for g in range(n_groups)` — per-group Python loop
- Lines 371-372: List comprehension splitting group into individual rows
- Lines 378-391: `while len(current) > 1` — binary tree reduction with host orchestration
- Lines 411-418: `for arr in arrays` — host concat

This is used in the overlay difference path: union all overlapping right geometries before differencing with left.

## Goal

Replace host-side tree reduction with device-side batched overlay. No per-group Python loop. All intermediate results stay on device.

## Implementation

### Approach: Batched binary tree reduction on device
1. Flatten all groups' geometries into a single OwnedGeometryArray with group membership array
2. For each round of the binary tree:
   - Pair up geometries within each group (CuPy indexing)
   - Batch all pairs across all groups into one call to binary_constructive_owned("union", ...)
   - Result replaces paired geometries for next round
3. `ceil(log2(max_group_size))` rounds total
4. Each round is one GPU overlay dispatch (not one per group)
5. Final result: one geometry per group, all device-resident

### Intermediate concat:
- Use device-level OwnedGeometryArray concatenation (similar to Phase 18's scatter)
- No host materialization between rounds

### Result:
- _concat_union_results operates on device-resident arrays
- Returns device-resident OwnedGeometryArray

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/kernels/constructive/segmented_union.py` — Replace _segmented_union_gpu host loops

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Per-group Python loop (lines 317-342) eliminated
- [ ] Binary tree reduction (lines 378-391) runs as batched GPU overlay
- [ ] Host concat loop (lines 411-418) eliminated
- [ ] Each round dispatches one batched GPU overlay call, not per-group calls
- [ ] All intermediate results stay device-resident
- [ ] Result is device-resident OwnedGeometryArray
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k "union or overlay" -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. _concat_owned_arrays buffer-level concat, to_shapely eliminated from union reduction.

title: Phase 2: Device-Resident Spatial Index Results
description:
## Context

This is Phase 2 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 1 (device-resident metadata).

The spatial index GPU path generates candidate pairs on device (CuPy arrays), then copies them to host at the ADR-0036 boundary. The overlay API then passes these host arrays to take() which re-uploads them to device via cp.asarray(). This is a completely avoidable D→H→D round-trip.

Currently:
- query.py line 133: `left_idx, right_idx = regular_grid_box_pairs.to_host()` — D→H
- query.py line 256: Same for point-tree box path
- query.py line 298: Same for regular-grid point index path
- _intersecting_index_pairs() in overlay.py returns numpy arrays
- device_take() then does cp.asarray(indices) — H→D re-upload

## Goal

When the GPU spatial index was used and the consumer is device-capable, return device-resident CuPy index arrays. Eliminate the D→H→D round-trip for index arrays in the overlay pipeline.

## Implementation

### Changes to spatial/query_types.py:
1. Add `DeviceSpatialJoinResult` dataclass with CuPy `d_left_idx`, `d_right_idx` arrays
2. `SpatialJoinResult.to_device()` / `.to_host()` methods

### Changes to spatial/query.py:
1. `query_spatial_index()` gains a `return_device=bool` parameter (default False for backward compat)
2. When `return_device=True` and GPU index was used, skip the `.to_host()` calls and return device-resident result
3. When GPU index was not used, return host arrays as before

### Changes to api/sindex.py:
1. `SpatialIndex.query()` gains `return_device=True` option
2. Owned-dispatch query path passes through device arrays when available

### Changes to api/tools/overlay.py:
1. `_intersecting_index_pairs()` gains an `owned_dispatch=True` path that returns device arrays when both sides have owned backing and GPU index was used
2. Falls back to host arrays when GPU unavailable

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/spatial/query_types.py` — DeviceSpatialJoinResult
- `src/vibespatial/spatial/query.py` — return_device parameter, skip D→H
- `src/vibespatial/api/sindex.py` — pass-through device arrays
- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — _intersecting_index_pairs device path

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `query_spatial_index(return_device=True)` returns CuPy arrays when GPU index was used
- [ ] `_intersecting_index_pairs()` returns device arrays when both DataFrames have owned backing
- [ ] No D→H transfer of index arrays when both sides are device-resident
- [ ] Backward compatibility: existing callers without return_device still get numpy arrays
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k "sindex or spatial" -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. DeviceSpatialJoinResult, device index pass-through, D→H→D round-trip eliminated.

title: Phase 20: Remove 10K Row CPU Threshold
description:
## Context

This is Phase 20 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 15 (GPU-primary face assembly).

At gpu.py lines 3313-3316:
```python
_GPU_OVERLAY_MAX_ROWS = 10_000
total_rows = left.row_count + right.row_count
if requested is ExecutionMode.AUTO and total_rows > _GPU_OVERLAY_MAX_ROWS:
    return _overlay_owned(left, right, operation=operation, dispatch_mode=ExecutionMode.CPU)
```

This hard-coded threshold forces CPU dispatch for any overlay with more than 10K total rows. For real-world datasets (which are commonly 100K-1M+ rows), this makes the GPU overlay pipeline dead code. The threshold was based on benchmark data showing the GPU topology construction was slower than Shapely for large inputs, but after Phases 7-15 eliminate all serial bottlenecks, this is no longer valid.

## Goal

Remove the artificial row-count threshold. GPU overlay handles all sizes when GPU is available.

## Implementation

### Changes to overlay/gpu.py — _overlay_owned:
1. Remove `_GPU_OVERLAY_MAX_ROWS` constant (line 3313)
2. Remove the threshold check (lines 3314-3316)
3. For ExecutionMode.GPU: no size check at all
4. For ExecutionMode.AUTO: base decision on GPU availability and input residency, not arbitrary row count
   - If input is already device-resident → GPU (no transfer cost)
   - If input is host-resident → let adaptive runtime decide based on actual kernel characteristics

### Important note:
Do NOT replace with a different threshold. The zero-copy pipeline should be capable of handling any size. If there are performance concerns for very large datasets, those should be addressed by optimizing the GPU pipeline, not by falling back to CPU.

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — Remove threshold at lines 3313-3316

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_GPU_OVERLAY_MAX_ROWS` constant removed
- [ ] Threshold check removed — GPU overlay available for all dataset sizes
- [ ] ExecutionMode.GPU works for datasets > 10K rows
- [ ] ExecutionMode.AUTO considers input residency, not row count
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. _GPU_OVERLAY_MAX_ROWS removed, GPU overlay available for all dataset sizes.

title: Phase 21: Non-Polygon Binary Constructive GPU Kernels
description:
## Context

This is Phase 21 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 9 (device segment pipeline). Can proceed in parallel with many other phases.

Currently binary_constructive.py only has GPU paths for:
- Point-Polygon: PIP kernel for intersection/difference
- Polygon-Polygon: overlay pipeline for all operations

All other family combinations return None from _binary_constructive_gpu() (line 346), forcing CPU fallback to Shapely. For a "0 CPU fallbacks required" pipeline, every family pair needs a GPU kernel.

### Missing GPU kernels:
1. LineString-Polygon intersection/difference — clipping line segments against polygon rings
2. LineString-LineString intersection — segment-segment intersection points/overlaps
3. Point-Point intersection/difference/union — coordinate equality comparison
4. Point-LineString intersection/difference — point-to-segment distance with zero tolerance
5. MultiPoint-Polygon intersection/difference — batched PIP over all points, compact valid
6. Point-Polygon union — trivial (returns GeometryCollection or polygon)
7. LineString-Polygon union — trivial (returns polygon if line inside, collection otherwise)

## Goal

Implement GPU kernels for all geometry family pair combinations used in overlay. No family pair falls through to Shapely CPU.

## Implementation

### LineString-Polygon clip kernel (`linestring_polygon_clip`):
- Sutherland-Hodgman adapted for line segments against polygon boundary
- 1 thread per segment of the linestring
- Count-scatter pattern: count output segments, then scatter clipped results
- Handles both intersection (keep inside) and difference (keep outside)
- Output: device-resident OwnedGeometryArray with LineString/MultiLineString family

### LineString-LineString intersection kernel (`linestring_linestring_intersect`):
- Segment-segment intersection computation
- 1 thread per segment pair (after bounds filtering)
- Output: intersection points (Point family) or overlapping segments (LineString family)
- Count-scatter for variable-size output

### Point-Point constructive kernel (`point_point_constructive`):
- 1 thread per row
- Coordinate equality comparison (within floating-point tolerance)
- intersection: keep if equal; difference: keep if not equal; union: keep both (deduplicate)
- Trivial kernel, ~20 lines

### Point-LineString constructive kernel (`point_linestring_constructive`):
- 1 thread per row
- Point-to-segment minimum distance computation
- intersection: keep point if distance == 0; difference: keep if distance > 0
- Reuse distance infrastructure

### MultiPoint-Polygon constructive kernel (`multipoint_polygon_constructive`):
- 1 thread per point within MultiPoint
- Batch PIP kernel over all constituent points
- Compact valid points via compact_indices
- Rebuild MultiPoint from compacted coordinates

### Changes to binary_constructive.py:
1. _binary_constructive_gpu handles all family pairs
2. Register new kernel variants in kernel registry
3. Each kernel returns device-resident OwnedGeometryArray

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/binary_constructive.py` — Handle all family pairs
- New kernel files in src/vibespatial/kernels/constructive/ for each kernel
- src/vibespatial/runtime/kernel_registry.py — Register new variants

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] LineString-Polygon intersection/difference GPU kernel exists and passes tests
- [ ] LineString-LineString intersection GPU kernel exists and passes tests
- [ ] Point-Point constructive GPU kernel exists and passes tests
- [ ] Point-LineString constructive GPU kernel exists and passes tests
- [ ] MultiPoint-Polygon constructive GPU kernel exists and passes tests
- [ ] _binary_constructive_gpu never returns None for any family pair
- [ ] All kernels return device-resident OwnedGeometryArray
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k "constructive or binary" -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] Results match Shapely output for all family combinations
close_reason: Landed. 11 GPU kernels for all non-polygon family pairs, _binary_constructive_gpu no longer returns None.

title: Phase 22: GPU Collection Extract
description:
## Context

This is Phase 22 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 1 (device metadata).

_collection_extract() at overlay.py lines 571-630 is called at overlay exit when keep_geom_type=True (the default). It uses:
- .explode() to decompose GeometryCollections into individual geometries
- .dissolve() to re-aggregate by source row
- Both are CPU/Shapely operations requiring full D→H materialization

This is the last operation in the overlay pipeline before returning to the user, so even if everything else is device-resident, this step forces a D→H round-trip.

## Goal

Implement a device-resident collection extract that operates on OwnedGeometryArray directly, filtering by geometry family tag without Shapely materialization.

## Implementation

### New function: _collection_extract_owned(owned, target_families)
1. Read tags array (device-resident from Phase 1)
2. Build a mask: `keep_mask = cp.isin(d_tags, target_family_tags)`
3. For non-collection rows: keep/reject based on tag
4. For GeometryCollection rows: decompose by reading family buffers, filter by target family, compact
5. Re-aggregate: group by source row, concat family buffers → rebuild MultiPolygon/MultiLineString as needed
6. Use `compact_indices` (CCCL) for efficient filtering

### The key insight:
GeometryCollections in OwnedGeometryArray are already stored with per-family buffers. Filtering by family is just masking + compaction on the tags array — no need to explode/dissolve.

### Changes to overlay.py:
1. When result has owned backing: call _collection_extract_owned
2. When no owned: existing _collection_extract path
3. Result stays device-resident

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — New _collection_extract_owned, wire into _collection_extract
- `src/vibespatial/geometry/owned.py` — May need family-level filtering helpers

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] _collection_extract_owned operates on device-resident OwnedGeometryArray
- [ ] Filters by geometry family tag without Shapely materialization
- [ ] GeometryCollection decomposition works at buffer level
- [ ] Result is device-resident OwnedGeometryArray
- [ ] No .explode() or .dissolve() calls when owned backing available
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. _collection_extract_owned filters by family tag at buffer level, no explode/dissolve.

title: Phase 23: Device-Resident Overlay API Wiring
description:
## Context

This is Phase 23 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phases 17 (make_valid wiring), 18 (device difference), 19 (GPU segmented union), 20 (no threshold), 21 (non-polygon kernels), 22 (GPU collection extract).

This is the integration phase. All individual components are device-resident (Phases 1-22). Now wire them together so the overlay API returns a GeoDataFrame with device-resident geometry backing end-to-end.

Currently each _overlay_* function may break device residency at intermediate steps:
- _overlay_intersection: may materialize via GeoSeries wrapping
- _overlay_difference: materializes via to_shapely (fixed by Phase 18)
- _overlay_symmetric_diff: two sub-differences, concat — may break
- _overlay_union: intersection + symmetric_diff, concat — may break
- _overlay_identity: intersection + difference, concat — may break

## Goal

All overlay operations return device-resident geometry arrays. The GeoDataFrame wraps a GeometryArray backed by device-resident OwnedGeometryArray.

## Implementation

### Changes to each _overlay_* function:

1. **_overlay_intersection**: 
   - binary_constructive_owned returns device-resident
   - Wrap directly in GeoSeries(GeometryArray.from_owned(result_owned)) — preserves device backing
   - No intermediate Shapely materialization

2. **_overlay_difference**: Already fixed by Phase 18

3. **_overlay_symmetric_diff**:
   - Both sub-differences return device-resident
   - Device-level concat of two OwnedGeometryArrays
   - No pandas geometry column manipulation that breaks owned backing

4. **_overlay_union**:
   - Intersection + symmetric_diff both device-resident
   - Device-level concat
   - GeoDataFrame wraps device-resident geometry

5. **_overlay_identity**:
   - Intersection + difference both device-resident
   - Device-level concat

### Device concat helper:
- Need `OwnedGeometryArray.concat(arrays)` — concatenates multiple device-resident arrays
- Concatenates per-family buffers, validity, tags, family_row_offsets
- All on device when all inputs are device-resident

### GeometryArray / GeoDataFrame integration:
- GeometryArray.from_owned preserves device backing (already works)
- GeoDataFrame construction with device-backed GeometryArray (verify no eager materialization)
- pd.concat on GeoDataFrames must preserve owned backing on geometry column

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — All _overlay_* functions
- `src/vibespatial/geometry/owned.py` — OwnedGeometryArray.concat class method
- `src/vibespatial/api/geometry_array.py` — Verify from_owned and concat preserve device backing

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] _overlay_intersection returns device-resident geometry
- [ ] _overlay_difference returns device-resident geometry (Phase 18)
- [ ] _overlay_symmetric_diff returns device-resident geometry
- [ ] _overlay_union returns device-resident geometry
- [ ] _overlay_identity returns device-resident geometry
- [ ] OwnedGeometryArray.concat exists and operates device-level
- [ ] GeoDataFrame geometry column carries device-resident backing to caller
- [ ] No intermediate Shapely materialization in any overlay operation
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
- [ ] End-to-end: device input → overlay → verify output geometry is device-resident
close_reason: Landed. OwnedGeometryArray.concat, _concat_same_type owned path, 5 overlay API fixes for owned preservation. 3 pre-existing tests fixed.

title: Phase 24: Remove CPU Fallback Requirements
description:
## Context

This is Phase 24 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 23 (device API wiring). This is the final phase.

After all previous phases, CPU fallback paths should be unreachable when GPU is available and input is device-resident. But the fallback code still exists and could be accidentally triggered, masking regressions.

This phase audits and guards all remaining CPU fallback paths so they are never silently reached in GPU mode.

## Goal

When GPU is available and input is device-resident, no CPU fallback path is reachable. Any accidental CPU fallback raises an error or warning rather than silently degrading.

## Implementation

### Audit and guard each fallback path:

1. **overlay/gpu.py — _overlay_owned (lines 3266-3292)**:
   - ExecutionMode.CPU path: keep for explicit CPU requests only
   - When dispatch_mode=GPU and input is device-resident: assert GPU path succeeds
   - Remove implicit CPU fallback for AUTO mode with CuPy available

2. **binary_constructive.py — binary_constructive_owned (line 441)**:
   - When dispatch_mode=GPU and GPU kernel returns None: raise RuntimeError instead of silent CPU fallback
   - After Phase 21, GPU kernel should never return None for any family pair

3. **overlay.py — _overlay_intersection (lines 127-136)**:
   - NotImplementedError catch → Shapely fallback: after Phase 21, this should never trigger
   - Add warning/assert in GPU mode

4. **overlay.py — _overlay_difference (lines 230-256)**:
   - `result_geoms is None` Shapely fallback: after Phase 18, owned path always succeeds
   - Guard with assert in GPU mode

5. **make_valid_pipeline.py — shapely.make_valid fallback**:
   - After Phase 17, GPU make_valid handles all cases
   - Guard with assert/warning in GPU mode

### Mark all remaining Shapely fallback paths:
- Add `# CPU-only mode` comments with clear `if requested is ExecutionMode.CPU` guards
- Any non-guarded Shapely call is a bug

### Add instrumentation:
- Instrument `copy_device_to_host` with a thread-local counter
- Add test: `with assert_zero_d2h_transfers(): overlay(df1, df2)` for device-resident inputs
- Any D→H transfer in GPU mode (except scalar sizes for allocation) is a test failure

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — Guard CPU fallback in _overlay_owned
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/binary_constructive.py` — Guard CPU fallback
- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — Guard CPU fallbacks in all _overlay_* functions
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_pipeline.py` — Guard shapely.make_valid fallback
- Test files — Add zero-D2H-transfer assertion tests

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No CPU fallback path reachable when GPU available and input device-resident
- [ ] dispatch_mode=GPU with device input → any CPU fallback raises error
- [ ] All remaining Shapely calls guarded by explicit `if ExecutionMode.CPU` checks
- [ ] copy_device_to_host instrumented with counter for testing
- [ ] Integration test: device input → overlay → zero D→H transfers in geometry pipeline
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -q` passes (full suite)
- [ ] End-to-end benchmark: device-resident input → overlay → verify device-resident output
- [ ] Profile with nsys: zero cudaMemcpy D→H in geometry pipeline
close_reason: Landed. D2H transfer counter + assert_zero_d2h_transfers, all Shapely fallbacks guarded with record_fallback_event, CPU-only mode markers.

title: Phase 3: Device-to-Device Take
description:
## Context

This is Phase 3 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 1 (device metadata) and Phase 2 (device index results).

With device indices from Phase 2 and device metadata from Phase 1, take() can stay entirely on device without any H→D re-upload of indices or D→H download of metadata.

Currently in geometry/owned.py, device_take():
- Line ~465: `d_indices = cp.asarray(indices)` — H→D re-upload if indices are numpy
- Lines 518-520: copies validity/tags/family_row_offsets D→H (eliminated by Phase 1)
- Result OwnedGeometryArray always has host metadata arrays populated

## Goal

When indices are already CuPy arrays (from Phase 2's device spatial index), device_take() accepts them directly with zero copies. Combined with Phase 1, the entire take() operation stays on device.

## Implementation

### Changes to geometry/owned.py:
1. `device_take()` checks if indices are already CuPy — skips `cp.asarray()` if so
2. `take()` detects CuPy indices and routes directly to `device_take()` without converting to numpy first
3. Result `OwnedGeometryArray` has `residency=DEVICE` with None host arrays (Phase 1 lazy pattern)

### Changes to api/tools/overlay.py:
1. `_overlay_intersection()`, `_overlay_difference()`: pass device index arrays directly to `take()` when available from Phase 2
2. Remove `np.asarray(idx1)` / `np.asarray(idx2)` wrapping when indices are already device arrays

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/geometry/owned.py` — device_take() CuPy detection
- `src/vibespatial/api/tools/overlay.py` — pass device arrays to take()

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `device_take()` accepts CuPy arrays without H→D re-upload
- [ ] `take()` routes CuPy indices to device_take() automatically
- [ ] Overlay intersection/difference paths pass device indices directly
- [ ] No H→D re-upload of index arrays in the overlay pipeline
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k "take or gather" -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. CuPy index detection in take()/device_take(), overlay passes device indices directly.

title: Phase 4: GPU make_valid — Self-Intersection Detection Kernel
description:
## Context

This is Phase 4 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 1 (device metadata). Independent of Phases 2-3.

The make_valid pipeline detects self-intersections via shapely.is_valid() — a CPU Shapely call that requires full D→H materialization. This is in the critical path because overlay() calls _make_valid(df1) and _make_valid(df2) before any GPU work starts.

Currently in make_valid_pipeline.py:
- Line 344: `shapely.is_valid(subset)` in `_detect_self_intersections_gpu()` — refines GPU mask with Shapely
- Line 438: `shapely.is_valid(geometries)` — fallback when GPU detection unavailable
- Line 453: `shapely.is_valid(geometries[non_polygon_rows])` — non-polygon validity
- Lines 601-606: Multiple `shapely.is_valid()` calls for validity detection

## Goal

Replace all shapely.is_valid() calls in the make_valid pipeline with a GPU self-intersection detection kernel. No Shapely dependency in the validity detection path.

## Implementation

### New NVRTC kernel: `detect_self_intersections`
- Reuses existing `classify_segment_pairs` infrastructure from segment_primitives.py
- Per-polygon: extract ring segments, generate intra-ring pairs, classify crossings
- A polygon is self-intersecting if any pair of non-adjacent segments has a proper crossing
- 1 thread per polygon (or per segment pair for larger polygons)
- Output: boolean mask `is_self_intersecting[row]`

### Changes to constructive/make_valid_pipeline.py:
1. `_detect_self_intersections_gpu()` stops calling `shapely.is_valid()` for refinement — uses GPU kernel only
2. New `gpu_is_valid_polygon(owned: OwnedGeometryArray) -> cp.ndarray` function that returns device-resident boolean mask
3. Replace all `shapely.is_valid()` calls with GPU kernel dispatch
4. Non-polygon validity (points, linestrings): these are always valid by construction — skip validation or use trivial GPU check

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_pipeline.py` — Replace shapely.is_valid calls
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_gpu.py` — New GPU detection kernel
- New kernel file or addition to existing kernel module for detect_self_intersections

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `detect_self_intersections` NVRTC kernel exists and correctly identifies self-intersecting polygons
- [ ] `_detect_self_intersections_gpu()` no longer calls `shapely.is_valid()`
- [ ] No `shapely.is_valid` calls remain in make_valid_pipeline.py critical path
- [ ] GPU validity detection returns device-resident boolean mask
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_make_valid_gpu.py -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_is_valid_ogc.py -q` passes
- [ ] Results match Shapely's is_valid for all test geometries
close_reason: Landed. GPU self-intersection detection via classify_segment_pairs_v2, shapely.is_valid eliminated from critical path.

title: Phase 5: GPU make_valid — Structural Repairs
description:
## Context

This is Phase 5 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 1 (device metadata). Independent of Phases 2-4.

The make_valid structural repair operations — ring closure check, duplicate vertex removal, ring orientation fix — currently use host-side Python loops and cp.asnumpy calls.

Currently in make_valid_gpu.py:
- `_gpu_close_rings`: host loop checking first/last vertex per ring
- `_gpu_remove_duplicate_vertices`: host loop with numpy dedup
- `_gpu_fix_ring_orientation`: host loop with numpy shoelace
- ~8 cp.asnumpy calls, ~6 Python for-loops total across these functions

## Goal

Replace all host-side structural repair operations with GPU kernels. All ring validation and repair stays on device.

## Implementation

### New NVRTC kernel: `check_ring_closure`
- 1 thread per ring
- Compare first/last vertex coordinates
- Output: boolean mask `needs_closure[ring]`

### Duplicate removal:
- `cp.searchsorted` for vertex→ring mapping
- `compact_indices` (existing CCCL primitive) for compaction
- No host loop needed

### Orientation fix:
- Activate existing `compute_ring_shoelace` kernel (currently dead code at make_valid_gpu.py line 125!)
- `segmented_reduce_sum` for per-ring signed area
- CuPy element-wise for orientation decision (flip if exterior CCW or hole CW)
- Coordinate reversal via CuPy fancy indexing within ring offset windows

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_gpu.py` — Replace host loops in _gpu_close_rings, _gpu_remove_duplicate_vertices, _gpu_fix_ring_orientation
- New kernel or kernel addition for check_ring_closure

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `check_ring_closure` kernel exists (1 thread/ring, ~15 lines CUDA)
- [ ] `compute_ring_shoelace` kernel activated (currently dead code)
- [ ] Ring closure, dedup, orientation all operate on device arrays only
- [ ] ~8 cp.asnumpy calls eliminated
- [ ] ~6 Python for-loops eliminated
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_make_valid_gpu.py -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k "valid" -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. check_ring_closure kernel, sync removal, launch_config, const __restrict__.

title: Phase 6: GPU make_valid — Segment Extraction and Pair Generation
description:
## Context

This is Phase 6 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 1 (device metadata). Independent of Phases 2-5.

make_valid segment extraction and intra-ring pair generation are 100% host-side: extracting ring segments into (x0,y0,x1,y1) tuples and generating all (i,j) pairs within each ring for self-intersection testing.

Currently in make_valid_gpu.py:
- Lines 549-589: Host segment extraction — Python loop over rings extracting segments
- Lines 613-641: O(n^2) host pair generation — nested Python loop generating segment pairs
- Lines 809-856: Host dedup + counting loops for split event processing

## Goal

Replace host segment extraction and pair generation with GPU kernels. Replace host dedup/counting with CuPy operations.

## Implementation

### New NVRTC kernel: `extract_ring_segments`
- 1 thread per segment
- Uses searchsorted for ring lookup (which ring does this segment belong to)
- Output: SoA arrays (x0, y0, x1, y1, ring_id, segment_id)
- ~25 lines CUDA

### New NVRTC kernel: `generate_intra_ring_pairs`
- 1 thread per pair
- Pre-computed pair count per ring via formula n*(n-1)/2
- Prefix sum gives per-ring pair offsets
- Each thread decodes its (i,j) pair from its global pair index
- Output: (left_seg_id, right_seg_id) arrays
- ~35 lines CUDA

### Dedup and counting (CuPy, no kernel needed):
- Dedup: `d_sorted_keys[1:] != d_sorted_keys[:-1]` — CuPy mask
- Per-segment counts: `cp.bincount(d_sorted_seg_ids)`
- Ring sizes: `segmented_reduce_sum` on per-segment counts grouped by ring

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/make_valid_gpu.py` — Replace host loops at lines 549-641, 809-856
- New kernel additions for extract_ring_segments, generate_intra_ring_pairs

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `extract_ring_segments` kernel exists (1 thread/segment, ~25 lines)
- [ ] `generate_intra_ring_pairs` kernel exists (1 thread/pair, ~35 lines)
- [ ] Host segment extraction loop (lines 549-589) eliminated
- [ ] Host pair generation loop (lines 613-641) eliminated
- [ ] Host dedup/counting loops (lines 809-856) eliminated
- [ ] All operations produce device-resident arrays
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/test_make_valid_gpu.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. O(1) pair decode kernel, extract_ring_segments kernel, host fallbacks eliminated.

title: Phase 7: GPU List Ranking for Cycle-Ordered Face Walk
description:
## Context

This is Phase 7 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. No dependencies on other phases (can start independently).

The face walk in overlay_gpu.py uses a serial Python loop at lines 2226-2233 that walks next_edge_ids per face to determine cycle ordering. The next_edge_ids array is a permutation — face cycle decomposition is solvable in O(log n) depth via pointer jumping + list ranking. This eliminates ALL serial cycle-walking loops.

## Goal

Replace the serial Python face walk loop with a GPU list ranking kernel that determines each edge's position within its cycle in parallel.

## Implementation

### New NVRTC kernel: `list_rank_within_cycle`
- 1 thread per edge
- Each thread walks backward from its edge to the cycle root (edge whose id = face_id label), counting steps
- For typical overlay faces (3-20 edges), this completes in microseconds per thread
- Output: `rank[e]` = position of edge `e` in its cycle
- ~30 lines CUDA

### Post-kernel assembly (CuPy):
```python
packed_key = face_id.astype(cp.int64) * edge_count + d_rank.astype(cp.int64)
sorted_result = sort_pairs(packed_key, cp.arange(edge_count, dtype=cp.int32))
face_edge_ids = sorted_result.values  # cycle-ordered, contiguous per face
face_offsets = exclusive_sum(valid_lengths)
```

### Reused infrastructure:
- `sort_pairs` (CCCL) — cycle-ordered edge layout
- `exclusive_sum` (CCCL) — offset array construction

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — `_gpu_face_walk` function (around line 2108)
- New kernel for list_rank_within_cycle

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `list_rank_within_cycle` NVRTC kernel exists (1 thread/edge, ~30 lines)
- [ ] Serial Python face walk loop (lines 2212-2236) eliminated
- [ ] 3 cp.asnumpy calls in face walk eliminated
- [ ] Face edge ordering matches serial algorithm output exactly
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed in commit 0f67843. launch_config for 3 kernels, cp.asnumpy eliminated, cp.any D2H sync eliminated.

title: Phase 8: Device-Only Overlay Data Structures
description:
## Context

This is Phase 8 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 7 (GPU list ranking).

The overlay pipeline constructs four major data structures that currently copy all their arrays to host at construction time:
- HalfEdgeGraph: 10 cp.asnumpy/copy_device_to_host calls at lines 2012-2024
- OverlayFaceTable: 8 cp.asnumpy calls at lines 2331-2338
- SplitEventTable: Host-primary with device state as secondary
- AtomicEdgeTable: Already has lazy pattern but verify no eager triggers

## Goal

Make all overlay data structures device-primary with lazy host materialization. GPU-only consumers never trigger D→H copies.

## Implementation

### HalfEdgeGraph (overlay/types.py and overlay/gpu.py):
1. Stop copying 10 arrays to host at `build_gpu_half_edge_graph` construction
2. Add lazy `_ensure_host()` method (same pattern as AtomicEdgeTable)
3. Properties trigger D→H on first host access only
4. Device state is the primary, host is secondary/lazy

### OverlayFaceTable (overlay/types.py and overlay/gpu.py):
1. Stop 8 bulk cp.asnumpy calls at `build_gpu_overlay_faces` construction
2. Add lazy `_ensure_host()` for all face arrays
3. left_covered, right_covered stay device-resident (already partially lazy)

### SplitEventTable (overlay/types.py):
1. Make device-primary: SplitEventDeviceState is the authoritative state
2. Host numpy arrays become lazy (populated on first property access)
3. source_side, row_indices, part_indices, ring_indices derivation stays device-side

### AtomicEdgeTable:
1. Already has lazy pattern — verify overlay pipeline never eagerly accesses host properties
2. Audit all call sites in overlay/gpu.py that might trigger _ensure_host()

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/overlay/types.py` — HalfEdgeGraph lazy pattern, SplitEventTable device-primary, OverlayFaceTable lazy pattern
- `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py` — Remove eager D→H at construction of HalfEdgeGraph and OverlayFaceTable

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] HalfEdgeGraph construction has zero D→H transfers (10 eliminated)
- [ ] OverlayFaceTable construction has zero D→H transfers (8 eliminated)
- [ ] SplitEventTable is device-primary with lazy host materialization
- [ ] AtomicEdgeTable has no eager host triggers in overlay pipeline
- [ ] GPU-only consumers of these structures trigger zero D→H copies
- [ ] Host property access triggers lazy D→H (backward compat)
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/ -k overlay -q` passes
- [ ] `uv run pytest tests/upstream/geopandas/tests/test_overlay.py -q` passes
close_reason: Landed. HalfEdgeGraph/OverlayFaceTable/SplitEventTable lazy host materialization, 16+ D→H eliminated.

title: Phase 9: Device-Resident Segment Pipeline
description:
## Context

This is Phase 9 of the Zero-Copy Overlay Pipeline epic. Depends on Phase 8 (device-only overlay structs).

The segment classification pipeline copies 14 arrays to host after GPU classification (segment_primitives.py lines 2082-2095), then build_gpu_split_events copies 4 more (gpu.py lines 3017-3020), and build_gpu_atomic_edges copies 2 more (gpu.py lines 3167-3168). These 20 D→H transfers are the inputs to the reconstruction pipeline — if they start on host, everything downstream starts on host.

After Phase 8 makes the overlay data structures device-primary, these transfers become the remaining bottleneck: the data flows from device (GPU classification) → host (D→H copies) → device (overlay structs consume it). A pure D→D flow eliminates 20 transfers.

## Goal

Keep segment classification results, split events, and atomic edges on device throughout. The overlay reconstruction pipeline consumes device arrays directly.

## Implementation

### Changes to spatial/segment_primitives.py:
1. `classify_segment_intersections()` returns `SegmentIntersectionResult` with device-primary state
2. Add `SegmentIntersectionDeviceState` holding all 14 arrays as DeviceArray/CuPy
3. Lazy host materialization (same pattern as AtomicEdgeTable)
4. Remove 14 `copy_device_to_host` calls at lines 2082-2095

### Changes to overlay/gpu.py — build_gpu_split_events():

title: INFRA-05: Add branch-mutual-exclusion analysis to ZCOPY001
description:
**File:** `scripts/check_zero_copy.py`, specifically `check_pingpong_transfers()` at line 181.

**Problem:** ZCOPY001 finds D2H and H2D calls anywhere in the same function and pairs the first D2H with the next H2D. It does not check whether they are in mutually exclusive branches. In `query_utils.py:_filter_predicate_pairs_owned()`, the D2H (`.to_host()` at line 339 in the early-return branch for `predicate is None`) and the H2D (`cp.asarray()` at line 379 in the predicate-filter branch) are in mutually exclusive `if`/`else` code paths — the function returns at lines 344-362 before ever reaching line 379. They can never both execute in a single call.

**Fix:** Add basic branch-exclusion analysis to `check_pingpong_transfers()`:
1. When a D2H and H2D are found in the same function, check if they're in sibling branches of the same `if`/`else`/`elif` conditional
2. If D2H is in a branch that contains a `return` statement before the H2D's branch is reachable, do not report ZCOPY001
3. Use AST parent tracking: for each D2H/H2D call, walk up the AST to find the enclosing `If` node. If both calls have the same `If` parent but are in different branches (`body` vs `orelse`), they're mutually exclusive.

**Acceptance:**
- `query_utils.py:339` ping-pong violation is no longer reported
- Real ping-pongs in the same control flow path are still flagged
- `uv run python scripts/check_zero_copy.py --all` violation count decreases by 1
- `uv run pytest -x -q` passes
close_reason: Closed

title: FIX-12: Eliminate csv_gpu.py:872 D2H — GPU hex-to-WKB decode kernel
description:
**Violation:** ZCOPY001 at line 872.

**Problem:** `_extract_wkb_and_parse()` downloads the entire byte array to host for `bytes.fromhex()` / `decode("ascii")` — CPU-only hex decoding.

**Fix:** NVRTC kernel for GPU hex-to-binary conversion:
1. Input: device byte array of ASCII hex characters, start/end offset arrays
2. Kernel: each thread processes one WKB row — convert hex char pairs to bytes via lookup table
3. Output: device byte array of raw WKB binary, ready for direct WKB parsing on device
Simple kernel (< 30 lines CUDA), high parallelism, no branches.

**Acceptance:**
- The bulk D2H transfer at line 872 (`cp.asnumpy` of byte data) is eliminated
- Hex decode runs entirely on GPU via NVRTC kernel
- WKB bytes stay on device for subsequent parsing — no new D2H introduced
- All downstream WKB consumers receive device-resident byte arrays
- `uv run pytest -x -q` passes
- `uv run python scripts/check_zero_copy.py --all` violation count decreases by 1
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix gpu-constructive 38-48x regression: per-geometry serialization in overlay path
description:
gpu-constructive benchmark: GPU 10.4s vs Shapely 0.27s at 1M scale (38x slower).

## Root Cause (Investigated)
Same pattern as the clip-rect line regression (already fixed). Three serial Python bottlenecks in clip_rect.py polygon path:

1. **_extract_all_polygon_rings (lines 1633-1668)**: Per-coordinate Python loop — 5M float() + list.append() calls at 1M scale. ~40% of time. The buffer.x/y arrays are ALREADY contiguous numpy — no copying needed.

2. **Reassembly loop (lines 1750-1805)**: Per-geometry Shapely Polygon construction — 1M iterations building Python coordinate lists + Polygon() calls. ~40% of time.

3. **from_shapely_geometries (line 1812)**: Converts 1M Shapely objects back to OGA — redundant roundtrip. ~20% of time.

The GPU Sutherland-Hodgman kernel takes <1% of total time.

## Fix
Mirror the clip-rect line fix pattern (already landed):
1. Vectorized extraction: numpy slicing on buffer.x/y, np.repeat/np.diff for offsets
2. Direct OGA construction: build FamilyGeometryBuffer from GPU output arrays
3. Skip Shapely entirely (lazy factory for .geometries if needed)

Expected: 10.4s → <0.1s

## Files
- src/vibespatial/constructive/clip_rect.py — _extract_all_polygon_rings, reassembly loop, from_shapely_geometries

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gpu-constructive at 1M faster than Shapely (speedup > 1.0)
- [ ] No per-element Python loops in polygon clip hot path
- [ ] All clip tests pass
close_reason: Landed. True zero-copy polygon clip: device-resident buffers → kernel → device-resident OGA. No per-element Python loops, no Shapely, no D→H for coordinates.

title: PERF: point buffer GPU threshold (10K) too high for common transit/POI datasets
description:
## Problem

The point buffer GPU kernel threshold (`POINT_BUFFER_GPU_THRESHOLD = 10_000` in `src/vibespatial/constructive/point.py`) requires ≥10,000 points before dispatching to GPU. The site_suitability benchmark buffers 2,000 transit station points at radius 200.0 — 5x below the threshold — so it always falls to Shapely.

## Context

The point buffer GPU kernels (`point_buffer_round` / `point_buffer_quad1` in `point.py:62-128`) are simple NVRTC kernels that generate circle approximation vertices around each point. They are among the simplest kernels in the codebase.

The 10K threshold was presumably set to amortize kernel launch overhead. But with precompiled NVRTC (disk CUBIN cache via ADR-0034), launch overhead is ~0.5ms. At 2,000 points with default `quad_segs=8`, the kernel generates 2000 × 33 = 66K vertices in a single launch — well within the GPU's efficient operating range.

## Affected Benchmark

**site_suitability**: `transit.geometry.buffer(200.0)` on 2,000 points runs Shapely instead of GPU. The buffer step itself is only ~1.4ms (points are cheap to buffer), so the direct perf impact is small. However, the resulting polygons lack `_owned` backing, meaning the subsequent `sjoin(suitable, transit, predicate="intersects")` cannot use the GPU spatial index for the transit side.

## Required Changes

In `src/vibespatial/constructive/point.py`:

1. Lower `POINT_BUFFER_GPU_THRESHOLD` from 10,000 to a value informed by micro-benchmarking (likely 500-1000)
2. Run a quick benchmark at 500, 1000, 2000, 5000, 10000 points to find the actual crossover where GPU buffer matches Shapely buffer speed
3. Consider aligning with other GPU kernel thresholds in the codebase for consistency

## Files to Modify

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/point.py` — `POINT_BUFFER_GPU_THRESHOLD` constant (currently line ~640)

## Success Criteria

1. `point_series.buffer(200.0)` on 2,000 points dispatches to GPU (verify via dispatch event showing GPU selection)
2. Micro-benchmark: GPU point buffer at the new threshold is at least as fast as Shapely (no regression)
3. The resulting GeometryArray has `_owned` backing (so downstream sjoin can use GPU spatial index)
4. All existing buffer tests pass (`pytest tests/ -k buffer`)

title: FIX-18: Eliminate overlay/gpu.py:2884 scalar sync — upper-bound allocation for polygon output
description:
**Violation:** ZCOPY001 at line 2884 in `_build_polygon_output_from_faces_gpu()` in `src/vibespatial/overlay/gpu.py`.

**Problem:** `total_coords = int(cp.asnumpy(d_ring_coord_offsets[-1:] + ring_coord_counts[-1:])[0])` reads a single scalar from device to size the output coordinate buffer allocation at line 2892 (`cp.zeros(total_coords, ...)`). This is a host sync to determine allocation size.

**Fix:** Compute an upper bound from device-resident metadata without syncing. The total coordinate count is bounded by the sum of all ring coordinate counts, which can be computed with `cp.sum(ring_coord_counts)` — but we need a host int for allocation. Instead: over-allocate using an upper bound derived from input size (total input coords is known), fill with the scatter kernel, then trim to actual size using the device-computed offset as a slice end (CuPy slicing is device-to-device, no sync). Alternative: fuse the size computation into the preceding kernel's output.

**Acceptance:**
- The scalar D2H sync at line 2884 is eliminated
- Output buffer sized without host sync — either upper-bound allocation + trim or fused kernel output
- No new D2H transfers introduced in the function or callers
- All downstream coordinate consumers receive device arrays
- `uv run pytest -x -q` passes
- `uv run python scripts/check_zero_copy.py --all` violation count decreases by 1
close_reason: Closed

description:

## Zero-Copy Requirement
The decode path MUST stay on device. No numpy. No Shapely. If CuPy arrays come in, CuPy arrays go out. Any host materialization is a bug, not a feature.

## Investigation needed

title: PASS-01: Pass-through transforms — forward device metadata without D2H round-trip
description:
## Purpose

8 pass-through transforms (simplify, affine_transform, orient, reverse, remove_repeated_points, segmentize, set_precision, snap) modify only coordinates, not metadata. Today they call `owned.tags.copy()`, `owned.validity.copy()`, `owned.family_row_offsets.copy()` which forces host materialization of metadata, then `build_device_resident_owned` re-uploads it. The metadata was already on device in `owned._device_metadata` — this is a pointless D→H→D round-trip.

## Background

For chained transforms like `simplify().affine_transform().orient()`, each step forces metadata to host then re-uploads. With this fix, metadata stays device-resident across the entire chain — the compounding payoff is the real win, not the per-call savings.

## Target Files (all under `src/vibespatial/constructive/`)

- `simplify.py:311`
- `affine_transform.py:248`
- `orient.py:251`
- `reverse.py:169`
- `remove_repeated_points.py:337`
- `segmentize.py:376`
- `set_precision.py:98`
- `snap.py:805`

## Implementation

At each call site, replace host metadata forwarding with device metadata forwarding:

```python
dm = owned._device_metadata
if dm is not None:
    tags, validity, fro = dm.tags, dm.validity, dm.family_row_offsets
else:
    tags, validity, fro = owned.tags.copy(), owned.validity.copy(), owned.family_row_offsets.copy()

return build_device_resident_owned(
    device_families=new_device_families,
    row_count=owned.row_count,
    tags=tags, validity=validity, family_row_offsets=fro,
)
```

The `_device_metadata is None` fallback handles host-resident inputs (CPU mode). Requires INFRA-02.

## Success Criteria

1. Each of the 8 files uses device metadata forwarding when `_device_metadata is not None`
2. Host fallback preserved for non-device-resident inputs (tested with CPU-mode test)
3. At least one `strict_device_guard` test verifies no D2H on a device-resident input
4. `uv run python scripts/check_zero_copy.py --all` passes
5. `uv run python scripts/check_import_guard.py --all` passes

title: FIX-02: Eliminate D2H2D in exterior.py:106 — keep offset loop on device
description:
**Violation:** ZCOPY001 at line 106 in `_exterior_gpu()` in `src/vibespatial/constructive/exterior.py`.

**Problem:** Lines 106-107 download `geometry_offsets` and `ring_offsets` from device to host. Lines 114-131 loop over them in Python to compute `out_geom_offsets` (prefix sum of ring lengths) and `src_indices` (a gather index map: which source ring populates each output slot). Line 134 re-uploads `src_indices` as `d_idx`. This is a D→H→D round-trip through a Python loop that does prefix-sum and repeat-fill — both standard GPU primitives.

**Potential fix:** `cp.diff(geometry_offsets)` gives ring counts per geometry on device. `cp.repeat(cp.arange(n_geoms), ring_counts)` builds the gather index map. `cp.cumsum` produces offsets. All on device, no Python loop needed.

**Acceptance:**
- The D2H transfers at lines 106-107 are eliminated
- The H2D re-upload at line 134 is eliminated
- No new D2H transfers introduced in the function or callers
- All downstream consumers receive device arrays
- `uv run pytest -x -q` passes
- `uv run python scripts/check_zero_copy.py --all` violation count decreases by 1
close_reason: Closed

title: SCATTER-02: properties, interiors, boundary — construct scatter metadata on device
description:
## Purpose

properties.py, interiors.py, and boundary.py construct scatter metadata (tags, validity, family_row_offsets) on host using numpy operations. All three should use device-resident construction. boundary.py has a tag-remapping step (4 family→family mappings) that benefits from a fused NVRTC kernel rather than chaining CuPy boolean operations.

## Target Files

- `src/vibespatial/constructive/properties.py:1731`
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/interiors.py:236,292`
- `src/vibespatial/constructive/boundary.py:386-481`

## Implementation

### Shared pattern: per-family enumeration via `exclusive_sum`

All three files build `family_row_offsets` with the same pattern: for each output family, find rows with that tag and assign consecutive local indices. Instead of `np.flatnonzero` + `np.arange` per family, use the existing CCCL `exclusive_sum` primitive:

```python
d_mask = (d_out_tags == fam_tag).view(cp.int32)
d_cumsum = exclusive_sum(d_mask)  # existing cccl_primitives.exclusive_sum
# d_cumsum[i] = count of family members before row i
d_fro = cp.full(row_count, -1, dtype=cp.int32)
d_fro[d_out_tags == fam_tag] = d_cumsum[d_out_tags == fam_tag]
```

One `exclusive_sum` call per output family (typically ≤2-3 families per operation). Reuses existing infrastructure.

### boundary.py: fused NVRTC tag-remap kernel

boundary.py remaps 4 input families to 2 output families and masks Point/MultiPoint as invalid. This is 5+ boolean operations that should be a single-pass NVRTC kernel:

```cuda
extern "C" __global__ void boundary_remap_tags(
    const int8_t* __restrict__ src_tags,
    int8_t* __restrict__ out_tags,
    bool* __restrict__ validity,
    int n,
    int8_t poly_tag, int8_t mpoly_tag,
    int8_t ls_tag, int8_t mls_tag,
    int8_t pt_tag, int8_t mpt_tag,
    int8_t out_mls_tag, int8_t out_mpt_tag
) {
    int i = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;
    if (i >= n) return;
    int8_t tag = src_tags[i];
    if (tag == poly_tag || tag == mpoly_tag) {
        out_tags[i] = out_mls_tag;
    } else if (tag == ls_tag || tag == mls_tag) {
        out_tags[i] = out_mpt_tag;
    } else {
        // Point/MultiPoint → mark invalid, keep original tag
        out_tags[i] = tag;
        validity[i] = false;
    }
}
```

One kernel launch, one pass. Then `exclusive_sum` × 2 output families for fro construction. Total: ~3-4 kernel launches vs 10+ for chained CuPy.

Register warmup for `boundary_remap_tags` kernel at module scope (ADR-0034).

### properties.py and interiors.py

These have simpler tag patterns (no remapping, just partitioning by existing tags). Use:
1. Source `d_tags` from `owned._device_metadata.tags` when available
2. `exclusive_sum` per output family for fro construction
3. No NVRTC kernel needed — the tag values are unchanged, only fro needs computing

### All files: host fallback

When `owned._device_metadata is None` (CPU mode), fall back to existing numpy path.

### Note on `exclusive_scan_by_key`

CCCL Python bindings (`cuda.compute.algorithms`) do not currently expose `exclusive_scan_by_key`. If it becomes available, the per-family `exclusive_sum` calls could be replaced with a single `exclusive_scan_by_key(tags, ones)` that computes all family-local indices in one call. Until then, the per-family `exclusive_sum` approach (≤3 calls) is the pragmatic choice.

Requires INFRA-02.

## Success Criteria

1. boundary.py uses a fused NVRTC kernel for tag remapping + validity masking
2. All three files use `exclusive_sum` for per-family enumeration (not `np.flatnonzero` + `np.arange`)
3. No `np.full`, `np.arange`, or `np.flatnonzero` in the metadata path when device metadata is available
4. Host fallback preserved for CPU mode
5. Warmup registration for boundary remap kernel (ADR-0034)
6. `strict_device_guard` tests for all three GPU paths
7. `uv run python scripts/check_zero_copy.py --all` passes
8. `uv run python scripts/check_import_guard.py --all` passes
